# Correctness & Honesty-Under-Failure Checklist: Fleet Control Plane

**Purpose**: Requirements-quality validation ("unit tests for English") on the axes where this feature can **silently lie** or **silently degrade** — the design record's named primary risk
**Created**: 2026-07-16
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) · [plan.md](../plan.md) · [research.md](../research.md) · [data-model.md](../data-model.md) · [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist`. Every item interrogates **what is written** in the spec and its design artifacts — not the future code. An item passes when the *requirement* is complete, unambiguous, consistent, and measurable; it fails when the requirement leaves room for the system to be wrong without anyone noticing.

**Framing** — the design record names the risk this checklist exists to police:

> The failure mode is not a wrong transport; it is a system that cannot distinguish "pause requested" from "paused", "sent" from "received", "stale" from "disconnected", or "run complete" from "process died".

**Out of scope by operator decision** (do not raise): the dashboard scope cut (`design:feature/fleet-dashboard`); the B2 read-cap premise (settled by operator ground truth; `research.md` pins it against re-derivation); story priorities P1–P6 as ordering rather than slices.

## Ambiguity-Collapse — can two distinct conditions render as one state?

- [ ] CHK001 Are the three status axes (connection / liveness / execution) defined such that no consumer can obtain a single collapsed status from the plane? [Consistency, Spec §FR-029/030, data-model §Status]
- [ ] CHK002 Is "abnormally-disconnected" specified as a condition **distinct from** every `executionStatus` value, rather than as one of them? [Clarity, data-model §Status]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the distinct causes of socket closure (SIGKILL, shutdown, socket failure, protocol restart, process detach, **sidecar restart**) explicitly named as producing the *same* observation, so no requirement implies closure identifies a cause? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026/028]
- [ ] CHK004 Is "termination reason unknown" stated as the **recorded value**, rather than left as prose commentary a reader could implement as a guess? [Measurability, Spec §FR-026]
- [ ] CHK005 Are the requested-vs-applied states of `pause` specified as separately observable, with no requirement permitting "requested" to be reported as "applied"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-059, contracts/plane-client-api §C6]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the `delivered` → `received` → `applied` distinction defined such that each transition has an identified *source of truth* (who reports it), not merely an ordering? [Completeness, Spec §FR-050]
- [ ] CHK007 Is it explicit that stream replay position (`Last-Event-ID`) and command status are **separate state with separate advancement rules**, and is any requirement that could conflate them absent? [Consistency, Spec §FR-058]
- [ ] CHK008 Are the three gap classifications (lost / in-flight-retrying / never-sent) each given a **decidable** predicate, rather than a description that leaves two of them indistinguishable? [Measurability, research §R-04, data-model §Sequences]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the settle bound that separates "lost" from "in-flight" specified as **derived from the backoff schedule** rather than as an unexplained constant? [Traceability, research §PT-014, §Open items]
- [ ] CHK010 Are uplink health and archive health specified as **independently surfaced**, with an explicit prohibition on a single combined "store degraded" indicator? [Clarity, Spec §FR-074, contracts/sidecar-plane-protocol §C9]
- [ ] CHK011 Does every health state carry enough structure (which hop, pending counts, last success, last failure, last error) to answer *which hop* without inference? [Completeness, Spec §FR-074]
- [ ] CHK012 Is "temporarily uncommandable" specified as a **distinct reportable state** from healthy, for a run whose sidecar connection is lost while execution continues? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK013 Are stale and fresh derived artifacts made distinguishable **by construction** (revision-in-key), with no requirement depending on a purge or TTL to avoid staleness? [Consistency, research §PT-005]
- [ ] CHK014 Is the boundary between "live" and "finalized" data specified precisely enough to decide which reads hit the registry and which hit cloud storage? [Clarity, Spec §FR-072]

## Overclaim — does any requirement promise a guarantee the system cannot keep?

- [ ] CHK015 Are delivery semantics stated **plainly** as at-least-once transmission / idempotent ingestion / effectively-once registry application, with an explicit statement that exactly-once is **not** offered? [Clarity, Spec §FR-043]
- [ ] CHK016 Does any requirement, read in isolation, invite an exactly-once reading — e.g. by combining spooling + retry + dedupe + sequence without the disclaimer? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-037…043]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the acknowledgement that durable storage **may transiently contain duplicate attempts** present, and is the condition under which it does not (object naming) stated as the reason rather than assumed? [Completeness, Spec §FR-043, data-model §Storage]
- [x] CHK018 **Does any surviving text still promise the sidecar "must not exit holding an un-flushed spool"?** That guarantee is unachievable by any shutdown sequence (SIGKILL runs no code; Windows delivers no real SIGTERM) and was inverted to a crash-safe WAL. [Conflict, research §R-03 vs Spec §Edge Cases, §PT-003] — **FAILED at authoring; now fixed.** The stale promise survived in the Edge Cases bullet and PT-003; both amended to the WAL inversion with the falsifiable kill-9 form.
- [ ] CHK019 Is graceful shutdown explicitly demoted to a **latency optimization** rather than a correctness guarantee, so no downstream requirement leans on it? [Clarity, research §R-03]
- [ ] CHK020 Is the crash-safety promise stated in a **falsifiable** form (kill -9 mid-spool ⇒ no record loss on restart), rather than as "the spool is durable"? [Measurability, quickstart §Scenario 4]
- [ ] CHK021 Is the immutability claim bounded correctly — "**published event objects** are never mutated" rather than "completed runs are immutable" — given the late-event model contradicts the broader claim? [Clarity, Spec §FR-066]
- [ ] CHK022 Is the dedupe set explicitly characterized as an **optimization, not a correctness mechanism**, so no requirement is written as though correctness depends on its TTL? [Clarity, research §Dedupe]
- [ ] CHK023 Is `installationSequence` explicitly prohibited from carrying **causal meaning across concurrent runs**, rather than merely described as "emission order"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-041]
- [ ] CHK024 Is any claim of cross-host ordering absent, or explicitly qualified as not derivable from wall-clock? [Consistency, research §PT-013]
- [ ] CHK025 Is skew estimation specified as **diagnostic only, never auto-correcting**, so no requirement implies corrected timestamps are authoritative? [Clarity, research §PT-013]
- [ ] CHK026 Is `cancel` specified as **cooperative and non-timing-out** — a run that never reaches a boundary stays visibly `cancelling` — with no requirement implying a guaranteed stop? [Clarity, research §PT-011]

## Silent-Success Traps — a mechanism that appears to work while doing nothing

- [ ] CHK027 Is there a requirement that the CDN's cache **effectiveness is measured**, not inferred from correct data? JSON is not cached by default; without an explicit cache rule the hit rate is zero while the data stays correct — the shield looks like it works and shields nothing. [Gap, research §PT-004, quickstart §Scenario 5]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the 404-bypass-cache exception stated as a **requirement**, given a cached "doesn't exist" stalls sequence probing when the event lands moments later — and given everything else in the design caches forever, making this the single easy-to-miss carve-out? [Completeness, research §PT-005]
- [ ] CHK029 Is the requirement that **keepalive comment frames re-arm the read-idle watchdog** written explicitly, rather than left implicit in "keepalive keeps the stream alive"? Its failure presents as a mystery disconnect every ~45s. [Gap, contracts/sidecar-plane-protocol §C4]
- [ ] CHK030 Is transport keepalive explicitly stated to prove **nothing** about process health, so no requirement uses it as a liveness signal? [Clarity, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK031 Is the query-shape prohibition (FR-069) written to bind **the plane's own API surface**, not only its internal reads — i.e. is it impossible to satisfy the letter of the API contract while sharding the cache key? [Consistency, contracts/plane-client-api §C7]
- [ ] CHK032 Is "canned, low-cardinality" enforced by **key layout** (path-only) rather than by caller discipline, so an uncanned query is unrepresentable rather than merely discouraged? [Measurability, research §PT-004]
- [ ] CHK033 Is the isolation exception specified so it **cannot pass silently**? The constitution's probe snapshots only the fixture's outer repo, so writes to `$HOME` would go unobserved — passing for the wrong reason, which is worse than failing. [Gap, plan §Complexity Tracking]
- [ ] CHK034 Is there a requirement that the machine-local store is **redirected to a temp dir under test**, so a run cannot mint identity into a real developer's `$HOME`? [Gap, plan §Complexity Tracking, research §Testability]
- [ ] CHK035 Is the manifest's worst failure — **a lost manifest write is a lie of omission that nothing detects** — addressed by a requirement (reconciliation backstop), rather than left to the manifest's happy path? [Coverage, research §PT-004]
- [ ] CHK036 Is listing specified as **demoted, not deleted** — retained as an off-hot-path reconciliation mechanism — so a future reader does not remove the only thing that catches a lost manifest write? [Completeness, research §R-04, §PT-004]
- [ ] CHK037 Is the Transform Rule identified as a **security control** (absent it, the hostname is an open proxy to every other bucket on the origin) rather than as configuration trivia a reader might skip? [Clarity, research §PT-004]
- [ ] CHK038 Is the undici head-of-line hazard recorded as **inference requiring a test before the topology is fixed**, rather than as an established fact? [Assumption, research §Transport topology]

## The Dominant Constraint — is fail-open falsifiable, and is its boundary drawn?

- [ ] CHK039 Is "never degrade the tool it observes" expressed as a **measurable** criterion (wall-clock statistically indistinguishable from telemetry-disabled; output and exit code unchanged), rather than as a principle? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001/002]
- [ ] CHK040 Is the **full state matrix** enumerated for that criterion — plane unreachable, hanging, absent; sidecar absent, mid-restart, version-skewed — so "no degradation" is testable rather than aspirational? [Coverage, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK041 Is it explicit that **no timeout constant exists** on the CLI path, because there is no network operation to bound — rather than a timeout being left as an unstated implementation choice? [Clarity, contracts/local-socket-protocol §C1]
- [ ] CHK042 **Is the Principle V exception's boundary drawn precisely — what MAY be silent versus what MUST fail loud?** The declared violation permits silent telemetry loss; it must not license silence anywhere else. [Clarity, plan §Complexity Tracking]
- [ ] CHK043 Are the fail-loud obligations that survive the exception each stated as requirements — spool depth / uplink health, archive health, unknown-or-revoked token refused (never downgraded), unrestorable sequence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-074/088, research §R-02]
- [ ] CHK044 Is the asymmetry between short-verb (no buffer, drop) and long-run (bounded buffer) specified with a **stated rationale**, so it does not read as an inconsistency to be "fixed"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK045 Is the bounded in-memory buffer's bound identified as a pinned constant rather than left as "small"? [Measurability, Spec §FR-007, research §PT-014]
- [ ] CHK046 Is the sidecar's subordination stated as a requirement — a run **continues executing** and **never blocks** on reconnection — rather than as a design aspiration? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]

## Amendment Coverage — do the four plan amendments land consistently, with no stale text surviving?

- [x] CHK047 **R-01 (object key):** does any artifact still specify `{invocationSequence}-{eventId}.json`? The key must be zero-padded sequence alone with `eventId` inside the object. [Conflict, Spec §FR-063/064 vs data-model §Storage, research §R-01] — **FAILED at authoring; now fixed.** `data-model.md` carried the amendment but `spec.md` FR-063/064 still specified the defective key. Both now agree; the old key survives only as a cited rationale.
- [ ] CHK048 R-01: is the zero-padding **width** specified (or its derivation), rather than left as "padded"? [Measurability, research §PT-014]
- [ ] CHK049 R-01: is the rationale recorded (probing + lexicographic sort) so a future reader does not "simplify" `eventId` back into the key? [Traceability, research §R-01]
- [x] CHK050 **R-02 (sequence across restart):** is `installationSequence`'s durability across sidecar restart stated in the **requirements** (not only in research), given a reset makes every later event look like a regression and the plane would reject its own fleet's telemetry? [Gap, Spec §FR-039/042 vs research §R-02] — **FAILED at authoring; now fixed.** FR-039 now carries the durable/monotonic/never-reset clause plus the fail-loud case; FR-042 carries the high-water-mark gap classification (R-04) and a new FR-042a recording that dedupe is an optimization, not a correctness mechanism.
- [ ] CHK051 R-02: is the unrestorable-high-water-mark case specified as **fail-loud**, never a silent reset to zero? [Completeness, research §R-02]
- [x] CHK052 **R-03 (WAL inversion):** is the Edge Cases bullet and PT-003 text updated so no artifact still carries the unachievable promise? [Conflict, Spec §Edge Cases, §PT-003] — **FAILED at authoring; now fixed.** See CHK018.
- [ ] CHK053 **R-04 (gap classification):** is it explicit that gap detection **never** reads the object store — because classification makes the durable set sparse by design, so absence-of-object is not absence-of-event? [Clarity, research §R-04]
- [ ] CHK054 Are all four amendments reflected consistently across spec ↔ data-model ↔ contracts, with no artifact carrying superseded text? [Consistency, research §Findings]

## Requirement Completeness — conditions the design names but may not have specified

- [ ] CHK055 Are the spool's **drop policy** and cap specified — naming *what is discarded first* and requiring the loss to be observable rather than silent? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK056 Is the reconciliation window's length specified as a pinned constant **and** is what the fleet reports *during* the window defined? [Gap, Spec §PT-010]
- [ ] CHK057 Is the reconciliation window required to exceed sidecar restart + reconnect, given sidecar restart is the case that closes every socket while nothing died? [Traceability, research §PT-010]
- [ ] CHK058 Are the plane-restart requirements complete — registry is **derived, not authoritative**; sidecars re-announce — rather than leaving live-session recovery undefined? [Completeness, research §PT-007]
- [ ] CHK059 Are `config-push`'s safety requirements complete (schema version, validation, allowed-key set, apply-timing, persistence after invocation end, compare-and-set)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-060]
- [ ] CHK060 Is `reconcile`'s long-running lifecycle specified with results linked by `commandId`, so a single acknowledgement cannot stand in for it? [Completeness, Spec §FR-061]
- [ ] CHK061 Are supersession rules specified **per command**, with an explicit statement that no universal rule exists? [Clarity, Spec §FR-057]
- [ ] CHK062 Is fan-out specified as **never atomic**, with the response reporting targets/accepted/unavailable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-062]
- [ ] CHK063 Is redaction's **ordering** (before spooling) specified as load-bearing, with the byte-identity invariant (FR-049) stated as what makes replay sound? [Consistency, Spec §FR-047/048/049]
- [ ] CHK064 Is the redaction **policy** (deny-by-default; which fields; length caps) specified, not just the boundary? [Completeness, research §PT-008]
- [ ] CHK065 Is the requirement that the bearer token **never crosses the local socket** stated, with its rationale (Windows named pipes default to an Everyone-readable DACL)? [Traceability, contracts/local-socket-protocol §C2]
- [ ] CHK066 Is authorization specified as coming from the **`0700` parent directory** rather than the socket file mode, given POSIX makes no guarantee and BSD-derived systems may ignore socket permissions? [Clarity, research §PT-001]
- [ ] CHK067 Is the move-vs-clone tension recorded as a **decision with a stated cost** (`mv` re-mints; `reattach` is the escape hatch), rather than left as an unnoticed consequence of path-hash keying? [Assumption, research §Open items]
- [ ] CHK068 Is FR-087/SC-018's headless exercisability written as a **requirement**, such that a state reachable only through a nonexistent UI is a defect rather than a documentation gap? [Measurability, Spec §FR-087]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- An item **fails** when the requirement permits the system to be wrong without anyone noticing — that is the through-line of every category here.
- **CHK018, CHK042, CHK047, CHK050** are the highest-value items: each is a place where a *surviving* or *missing* sentence would license a silent lie.
- Items marked `[Conflict]` assert that two artifacts may disagree — resolve by amending the artifact, never by softening the requirement.

### Authoring-time run: 4 of 68 items were run and had already failed

The Amendment Coverage category was **run against the artifacts while this checklist was being written**, and it immediately caught a real inconsistency: the four plan amendments (R-01…R-04) had landed in `research.md` and `data-model.md` but **`spec.md` still carried the superseded text** — the defective object key, and the unachievable un-flushed-spool promise. `/speckit-analyze` would have found the contradiction later; the checklist found it first.

Fixed in the same pass: FR-063/064 (zero-padded sequence key, `eventId` inside the object), FR-039 (durable never-resetting sequence + fail-loud), FR-042 (high-water-mark gap classification), new FR-042a (dedupe is an optimization), the Edge Cases spool bullet, and PT-003 (WAL inversion). Each amendment cites its research finding inline, so the reasoning travels with the requirement rather than living only in `research.md`.

**The remaining 64 items are unrun** — they are for a reviewer, and the author is the wrong party to mark their own requirements as passing (`.claude/rules/agent-discipline.md`: the agent posts evidence; the operator decides).
